criteria.criterion: Criterion A: ≥5 of 9 symptoms during the same 2-week period, representing a change from prior functioning. At least one symptom must be (1) depressed mood or (2) loss of interest or pleasure (anhedonia). Symptoms: 1. Depressed mood most of the day... 2. Markedly diminished interest or pleasure... 3. Significant weight/appetite change... 4. Insomnia or hypersomnia... 5. Psychomotor agitation or retardation... 6. Fatigue or loss of energy... 7. Feelings of worthlessness or excessive/inappropriate guilt... 8. Diminished ability to think or concentrate, or indecisiveness. 9. Recurrent thoughts of death...
criteria.evidence: Patient reports 'pretty lethargic and hopeless lately' (suggests possible depressed mood and fatigue/loss of energy) and 'inability to concentrate, specifically can't concentrate worth shit lately' (matches symptom 8). Duration reported as 'past 30 days' exceeds 2 weeks, but only 3 symptoms mentioned so far; no report on anhedonia, weight/appetite, sleep, psychomotor changes, guilt, or suicidal thoughts. Need more symptoms and confirmation of 2-week period with change from prior functioning.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B: The symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.
criteria.evidence: Patient reports symptoms but no explicit statement of distress or impairment in functioning (e.g., work, relationships). 'Lately' and concentration issues imply possible impact, but not confirmed.

criteria.criterion: Criterion C: The episode is not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.
criteria.evidence: No patient report on substances or medical conditions; requires clinical judgment and history not available in phone intake.

criteria.criterion: Criterion D: The episode is not better explained by schizoaffective disorder, schizophrenia, schizophreniform disorder, delusional disorder, or other specified/unspecified schizophrenia spectrum and psychotic disorders.
criteria.evidence: No patient report on psychotic symptoms or related disorders; requires clinical judgment.

criteria.criterion: Criterion E: There has never been a manic or hypomanic episode (unless these were substance-induced or attributable to a medical condition).
criteria.evidence: No patient report on history of mania/hypomania; requires clinical judgment to rule out bipolar.

criteria.criterion: Criterion A: Depressed mood for most of the day, for more days than not, for at least 2 years (1 year in children/adolescents).
criteria.evidence: Patient reports symptoms 'for the past 30 days' only; far short of 2-year requirement.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B: Presence, while depressed, of two or more of: poor appetite or overeating; insomnia or hypersomnia; low energy or fatigue; low self-esteem; poor concentration or difficulty making decisions; feelings of hopelessness.
criteria.evidence: Patient reports 'lethargic and hopeless' (low energy/fatigue and feelings of hopelessness) and 'inability to concentrate' (poor concentration); meets at least 3 but duration too short for full criteria.

criteria.criterion: Criterion C: During the 2-year period, the person has never been without the symptoms in Criteria A and B for more than 2 months at a time.
criteria.evidence: Only 30 days reported; cannot assess 2-year persistence or gaps.

criteria.criterion: Criterion D: Criteria for a major depressive episode may be continuously present for 2 years.
Criterion E: There has never been a manic episode and the disturbance is not better explained by... [schizoaffective, schizophrenia spectrum, etc.].
criteria.evidence: No report on mania or psychotic disorders; requires clinical judgment.

criteria.criterion: Criterion F: The symptoms are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.
criteria.evidence: No patient report on substances or medical conditions; requires clinical judgment.

criteria.criterion: Criterion G: The symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.
criteria.evidence: No explicit report of distress or impairment.

criteria.criterion: Criterion A: The development of emotional or behavioral symptoms in response to an identifiable stressor(s) occurring within 3 months of the onset of the stressor(s).
criteria.evidence: Patient reports symptoms for 'past 30 days' (within 3 months possible) but no mention of any identifiable stressor.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B: These symptoms/behaviors are clinically significant as evidenced by either of the following: 1. Marked distress that is out of proportion to the severity or intensity of the stressor... 2. Significant impairment in social or occupational functioning.
criteria.evidence: Patient reports 'lethargic and hopeless' and concentration issues but no explicit distress level, proportionality to stressor, or impairment; no stressor identified.

criteria.criterion: Criterion C: The stress-related disturbance does not meet the criteria for another mental disorder and is not merely an exacerbation of a preexisting mental disorder.
criteria.evidence: Requires ruling out other disorders like MDD; clinical judgment needed.

criteria.criterion: Criterion D: The symptoms do not represent normal bereavement.
criteria.evidence: No mention of bereavement or loss.

criteria.criterion: Criterion E: Once the stressor or its consequences have terminated, the symptoms do not persist for more than an additional 6 months.
criteria.evidence: Symptoms ongoing for 30 days; stressor status and termination unknown.
